feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Services
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Services Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How can we use cloud identity service or custom Idp on local SAP btp?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
I have created a full stack CAP application with node.js with having XSUAA authentication.
Also have use Cloud identity service by which multiple user can login with their credential and use my app.
I have deployed my app and its working fine.
Any user on visiting the url it ask for idp login.

it we select custom then it redirect to custom idp login page.

SO i want to do same thing on the local sap btp at development level.
For that if i run the project locally with the command:
cds watch --profile hybrid and 
cds bind --exec -- npm start --prefix app/router (it is required because i declare auth type xsuaa )

It directly login with my default identity provider and

i want that it will ask to login with custom idp login on local sap btp at the development phase.

Thank you..

entry.title: SAP Enable Now und IAS
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have configured IAS as a proxy and am provisioning users from Azure to IAS and then to SAP Enable Now Azure->IAS->. How can roles be automatically assigned to provisioned users in Enable Now ?

Is there something similar to groups/teams in SAP Enable Now, like in SAC, that can be granted access via a role?

Unfortunately, I can't find any information on this.

Many Thanks for any help!

entry.title: SAP Cloud Identity Services and SAP ALM for business users
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM ; SAP Cloud Identity Services ; 

Dear SAP Community, 

I am working on getting more details and understanding of capabilities of SAP Cloud Identity Services through implementing cases focused on the integration of SAP Cloud Identity Services with local or customer identity providers, such as AD, ADFS, or local LDAP.

Currently, I would like to set the connection to SAP ALM for users who belong to a local identity provider (business users). We have many people who would like to work in ALM but I don't want to create manually the account for each user. The best is the user can login in ALM with the password of local identity provider and if the user belongs to the specific group in local Idp, then it should get a basic rights in ALM tenant. Specifically and most interesting, I am aiming to configure a scenario where users can log in to SAP ALM using their local identity provider passwords. From my understanding, there are three possible approaches:

  1. User provisioning operates in real-time, creating users upon their first connection to ALM. However, it appears that there is no target application related to ALM within IPS documentation.

  2. User provisioning operates in real-time, where the user is first created in IAS, with provisioning configured so that both source and target are set as IAS. A specific group can then be assigned to the user in IAS based on their group membership in the local identity provider. Using AD as a source in IPS is not good for us because we don't want to synch all AD users with IAS. *but may be we can use group as a filter to restrict the user list, however, AD as a source in IPS should be the last option if nothing else is available.

  3. Using the shadow user feature within the ALM tenant.

Questions:

  1. I have not found any documentation on configuring a scenario where a user can login to a BTP application using the login and password from a local identity provider. Is there guidance on this setup?

  2. Which of the three integrations above do you think would be the most effective for this task?

Thank you in advance for your feedback.

entry.title: I am using IAS to provision user from LDAP to SAP S/4
entry.content:
To avoid huge amount of users sync i want to use "ldap.group.filter" but it looks it does not work and it retrieve all users even if they are not member of LDAP groups I want to filter on.

 

Any idea ?

entry.title: Issue with Guest User Provisioning to SAP IAS
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are experiencing an issue where guest users are not being provisioned to SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS). The interface between Microsoft Azure and SAP IAS provisions all regular employees at 15-minute intervals; however, it does not provision external users, who are registered as guest users in Azure.

This limitation prevents us from managing guest user permissions within SAP IAS. While guest users can log in to applications such as the Build Workzone, we are required to manually assign their permissions directly on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

Additionally, these guest users appear on the BTP in a cryptic format as so-called shadow users. Instead of the standard user IDs, they are represented with an encrypted form. A screenshot from SAP BTP illustrating this issue is attached below.

Screenshot from SAP BTP

 

We kindly request your assistance in resolving this issue to ensure that guest users are properly provisioned to SAP IAS and their permissions can be centrally managed.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: IAS - sending different email templates per country
entry.content:
Hello,

we have a requirement for one of our clients to send different email templates depending on the country of the Onboardee.

We created a custom email template set for Onboardees and configured different versions depending on the language, but this depends on the language that the user's browser is set to (so if your browswer is set in English US, you get the email in English US etc.).

In this case we need to be able to send a different email template depending on the country of the onboardee. Example: the client employees in India have the browser set in English US, but we need to send a different text (still in English US) because the onboarding procedure in that country is different from other countries so they need to send different instructions.

is it possible to import the country of the onboardee and use it as a condition to trigger a different welcome email? how can we add the country in IPS transformations?

thanks

Marco

entry.title: SCIM API Filter is throwing an error when using SAP SuccessFactors schema extensions
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We want to change the userName attribute for the user in SAP SuccessFactors and want to use the SCIM API for this.
Between SAP SuccessFactors and our IAM, we have SAP Cloud Identity Services as a proxy.
The whole thing also works as long as we use the user ID which uses the SCIM API directly in the URL.

We don't have and don't want to keep this ID in the IAM, as it is something internal to SF and CIS.

With the SCIM API, it is also possible to query other attributes to read out a specific user.
This is done via the URL parameter "filter".

So we can read out a user via SCIM by going to the attribute “personIdExternal”.
This also works if the SCIM API end point is SAP SuccessFactors.

 

GET { 
url: https://<sf-data-center-tenant>/rest/iam/scim/v2/Users?filter=urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:successfactors:2.0:User:personIdExternal+eq+"1234567892"
body: json
auth: bearer
}

 

As a result, I get the user who has this “personIdExternal”.

As I have already mentioned, we still have SAP Cloud Identity Services between IAM and SAP SuccessFactors, which acts as a proxy

Unfortunately, this doesn't work.
The SCIM API end point looks like this:

 

GET { 
 url: https://xxxxxxxxxx.accounts.cloud.sap/ipsproxy/service/api/v1/scim/<sf-tenant>/Users?filter=urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:successfactors:2.0:User:personIdExternal+eq+"1234567892"
body: json
auth: basic
}

 

Here I get the following error:

 

{
  "schemas": [
    "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error"
  ],
  "detail": "Invalid filter value.",
  "status": 422
}

 

 From my point of view this should work or is this unsupported by SAP CIS?

Thanks for your tips and help 😄

best regards,
Andy

entry.title: How to assign Users as Approver and Requester in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I am now creating Approval Form using SAP Build Process Automation.

1. And I am confusing how can I assign users as Approver and Requester Roles.

2. And how can I give administrator role to user also?

Could you please give me advice about these questions?

And if you have any related documentation, cloud you please share with me?

 

Thank you.

Best Regards,

Aye Chan

entry.title: Is it possible to get a list of users in SAP BTP?
entry.content:
Hello,

in SAP BTP I use Cloud Identity Services, which has been integrated with AD via Identity Providers. In BTP, in the Security->Users after a successful login, a user is created as a shadow user. Is it possible to use some API to get a list of shadow users? Or get a list of users directly from AD?

Thank you for any help

entry.title: Joule Business scenarios - subscriber provider - IAS token issue
entry.content:
We are developing an integration for Joule business scenarios.
Here we are having a subscriber subaccount which communicates with provider subaccount and its Joule app.
We are using IAS based authentication for token exchange between subscriber and provider, facing authentication issue here. We have done the destinations setup, as well as created IAS app on the subscriber's end. When sending the request to provider's service and retrieving JWT token through OAuthJwtBearer token method in the provider service, we are getting following error:

 {"error":"invalid_target", "error_description":"Service provider has no consumed apis defined."}

If anyone has any insights or experience building similar integration, please do let us know. If there is any active community regarding this topic, please do let us know.

entry.title: Error During User Provisioning with IPS to Enable Now
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have provisioned the users from Azure to IAS and then to Enable Now. When I delete a user in IAS or in Azure (e.g., in the source system), the user is also deleted in the target system (Enable Now) through a provisioning job. However, when I try to provision the same user again in Enable Now, I get the following error:

user: f200d114-dfbe-4da4-942e-f93c3d0c18ef, system: Target Enable Now, time: 2024-11-21T13:09:06.302+00, error: HTTP operation failed invoking https://tenant-enable-now/scim/v2/Users with statusCode: 400, Response: {  "status" : "400",  "scimType" : "uniqueness",  "schemas" : [ "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error" ],   "detail" : "userUuid is or was already assigned" }, content: {  "active": true,   "emails": [{"value": "enable@mail.com"}],   "name": {"familyName": "enable", "givenName": "enable"},   "schemas": [     "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User",     "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User"   ],  "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User": {     "userUuid": "f200d114-dfbe-4da4-942e-f93c3d0c18ef"   },  "userName": "enable@mail.com" }

When I change, for example, the email address of a user in Azure or IAS (Source System) and provision the user to Enable Now, I get the following error:

user:U_8934VDGDIU9698CJAH7KJSKS, system=Targer Enable Now,  time=2024-11-21T1,  error=HTTP operation failed invoking https://tenant-enable-nowp/scim/v2/Users/U_8934VDGDIU9698CJAH7KJSKS with statusCode: 400, Response: {   "status" : "400",   "scimType" : "mutability",   "schemas" : [ "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error" ],  

"detail" : "User auth_name is immutable" }, content={"active":true,"emails":[{"value":"enable2@mail.com"}],"id":"U_8934VDGDIU9698CJAH7KJSKS","name":{"familyName":"enable3","givenName":"enable2"},"schemas":["urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User","urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User"],"urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User" :{"userUuid":"3dsfg45-sdsg45-d-9ff45176-sff4ycycv"},"userName":"enable3@mail.com"}

How can I provision the deleted users again to the Target System? How can I update the changed user attributes, such as the user email, in Enable Now with a provisioning job?


Many Thanks

Best Regards










​

entry.title: IAS for External Learner and Onboardee
entry.content:
Hello all,

We have recently faced an issue that may have been emphasised due to the enforcement of using IAS for External Learners.

Summary
Our customer was looking to onboard one of their external learners (addressed as "User") via the onboarding process, and both platforms/applications (Learning and Onboarding) are connected to the same IAS. This user can then be authenticated when using email address but cannot log in.

Process
This user is an active external learner using SAP IAS for authentication, the profile was registered with the user's first name, last name and email address. When the onboarding process started, the customer admin provided the same information recorded in the external learning profile, including first name, last name, and user's email address. The email address then leads to a user sync error in IAS, noting a profile already exists with the same email address.

Cause (Potentially?)
This user's email address already exists in IAS, the user can pass the IAS authentication but cannot log in successfully, given the external learning profile uses "firstname.lastname" as username and the onboarding profile uses "potential employee ID" as username. So the user cannot access the portal for onboarding processes.

Temp Fix
We suggested a temporary fix by asking the customer to deactivate this user's external learning profile from LMS. IAS will then delete this user's profile on IAS and free up the email address. Then Onboarding can utilise the email address and allow the user to finish the onboarding process. Once the user is onboarded and created as an employee, the customer admin can merge the 2 learning profiles together and retain the learning history.

Thoughts
However, the customer shouldn't need a "workaround", such as using a different email address or deactivating one of the IAS user profiles. It is a normal and reasonable process for any business to recruit external learners to become a part of the organization. IAS should have gone through the business impacts when pushing for SAP IAS.

Furthermore, if any customers are using "External Learning", "Onboarding", as well as "Pre Day 1 Learning", it may create chaos. The customer admin may have to constantly activate/deactivate different user profiles in IAS to allow users to access different portals, and it will lead to a massive amount of manual work for the admins.

Has anyone faced the same issue before? Or predict this may happen in the near future? Please kindly share your thoughts! (A ticket has been raised with SAP and may be requested to raise an enhancement request....)

Thanks,
Sunny

entry.title: SCIM User Deployment to IBP System, Role assignment via SAML token possible?
entry.content:
Hello,

We are using SAP IPS do deploy and authorize Users in SAP IBP.

The IBP users receive the role assignment through the SCIM deployment from the IAS, not from a SAML token that is transferred during login.
This presents us with the challenge that the search string in the IPS “aad.group.filter” becomes longer and longer the more roles there are in the IBP which need to be mapped to AD Roles, which slows down the graph query and makes administration more difficult.
Is it possible to assign roles to users via a SAML token when logging into the IBP?
In this case, we would only deploy the users to the IBP via the IPS and thus only have one role in the “aad.group.filter”.

The SAML token is issued by Azure via IAS Proxy functionality. (token size is no problem at the moment)

I assume IBP is based on a cloud S/4 System(I don't know the underlaying architecture by detail). I cannot find a feature to use SAML role mapping.

 

best regards

Bastian

entry.title: IPS: Email and Telephone Not Provisioned to Enable Now
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Why are the email and phone number attributes not being provisioned  to Enable Now using the default transformation code?

I am provisioning users with SAP IPS  from Azure to IAS and then to Enable Now.

Many Thanks

entry.title: IAS to SAC: User Updates Not Provisioned
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have provisioned users from IAS to SAC.

However, when I change a user in IAS (e.g., updating the last name) and run the Read job, the changes are not provisioned to the target system (SAC).

There is no error, but the users are read, and no update is performed in the target system.

What should I pay attention to and what needs to be configured to ensure that only the user changes are successfully provisioned?

 

Many Thanks

Best regard

entry.title: Best Practices for Managing Users and Groups in Azure and IAS
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Currently, we have the following scenario implemented: We have Azure as the IdP → IAS as a proxy → Cloud Apps. The groups are created in Azure, and users are assigned to those groups in Azure. These groups with their users are then provisioned to SAP IAS. From SAP IAS, the users/groups, such as SAC_Group, are provisioned to the SAC app, or the group ENABLE_Group is provisioned to Enable Now.

Since we, as the SAP team, do not have access to the Azure tenant, I am asking if there is a better approach or any best practices for managing users/groups.

Could we provision only users from Azure and create the groups directly in IAS, then manually assign users to these groups und then provision to Cloud Apps? Are there any disadvantages to this approach?

What are your experiences? How do you handle this in your environment? What are the best practices: Should users/groups be managed in Azure, or should groups be managed directly in IAS?

Which approach is better: Managing users/groups in Azure or provisioning users to IAS and managing the groups there?

Thank you so much!

Best Regards

entry.title: IAS Deletes Business User Group in SAP
entry.content:
When Syncing our Users from the IAS to SAP, the Business User Group of our Users is set empty.

We don't want IAS to do Group Governance within SAP.

I have already removed everything from the transformations, that included Group.

Why is it still deleting User Groups set in SAP?

THX!!!

entry.title: Successfactors initial deployment - Identity provisioning really needed?
entry.content:
Hi all,

we are initially deplyoment a 3 environment SF landscape. This came pre-bundled with one IAS DEV Tenant and re-used two other existing IAS tenants. So our setup is as such

  • IAS DEV = SF DEV
  • IAS QAS = SF QAS + IBP QAS
  • IAS PRD = SF PRD + IBP PRD

Now our implementation partner advises on provisioning of users from SF to IAS.

Is it really needed to have users synced from SF to IAS? I see it seems mandatory to some extend when a use case is People Analytics. However, there ought to be also some old school Sync using OData which functions directly between SF and PA?

Any clarficiation or confirmation would be nice.

Jens

entry.title: IPS - transformation - adding a user to a group using patch
entry.content:
Hi all.
In out IAS tenant users are created by replication from SF.
Now we have requirement to update nickname and groups for some users. It should be done by replication from ABAP system.
Base on docs https://help.sap.com/docs/identity-provisioning/identity-provisioning/patched-and-merged-attributes?q=contains  we have prepared a patch transformation. 
It works if only nickName is updated 

 

 

{
    "user": {
        "mappings": [
            {
                "sourcePath": "$",
                "targetPath": "$"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.id",
                "type": "remove"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.id",
                "sourceVariable": "entityIdTargetSystem"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.schemas",
                "type": "remove"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.schemas[0]",
                "constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.schemas[1]",
                "constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.schemas[2]",
                "constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.emails[0].primary",
                "condition": "$.emails[0].length() > 0",
                "constant": true
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.meta",
                "type": "remove"
            },
            {
                "constant": "userName",
                "targetVariable": "entityCorrelationAttributeName"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.userName",
                "targetVariable": "entityCorrelationAttributeValue"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.schemas[0]",
                "constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:PatchOp",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.meta",
                "type": "remove",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.Operations[0].op",
                "condition": "$.nickName EMPTY false",
                "constant": "add",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.Operations[0].path",
                "condition": "$.nickName EMPTY false",
                "constant": "nickName",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.nickName",
                "targetPath": "$.Operations[0].value",
                "condition": "$.nickName EMPTY false",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.Operations[1].op",
                "constant": "add",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            }
        ]
    }
}

 

 

The problem occurs when we want to update the group

following code is added to the transformation 

 

 

            {
                "targetPath": "$.Operations[1].op",
                "constant": "add",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.Operations[1].path",
                "constant": "groups",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.Operations[1].value",
                "constant": "xxxxxx-7f28-4b83-9aaf-xxxxxx",
                "scope": "patchEntity"
            }

 

 

And error is generated

Question, how to prepare correct transformation for user group update?

A.

entry.title: IAS/IPS: UPN in Azure and UserName in Cloud Apps
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

The following problem:

As far as I know, the UPN is typically set as an email address.

In my scenario, users are provisioned from Azure -> IAS -> cloud applications.

However, when provisioning users from IAS  (for example) to Enable Now, the usernames are created as email addresses.

 

 

How can I avoid this?

For example, in Entra ID Source System, the User Name is defined in the Default Code as follows:

{
"sourcePath": "$.userPrincipalName",
"targetPath": "$.userName",
"correlationAttribute": true
},

So, this means that the UPN (Azure) becomes the Login Name ( in IAS).

For example, if I map the employee ID to the username in the IPS transformation, the user is created in Enable Now with the employee ID (which would be ideal for us) as the username, but the users cannot authenticate in Azure with the employee ID.

Is there a way to change the UPN?

Or is there another solution to ensure that the usernames in cloud applications are not created as email addresses, while users can still authenticate in Azure?

Thank you very much !!!

Best regards
